prompt: I have an old thermostat with a serial interface — can you wrap it so I can control it with natural language commands?
Our legacy inventory database only has a CLI. Build a conversational layer over it so the warehouse team can query stock levels by asking questions.
I want my Home Assistant morning routine to trigger lights, coffee maker, and blinds all at once when I say good morning.
We have a bash script that generates reports but nobody remembers the flags. Can you put a natural-language front end on it?
Wrap our internal Jenkins build system so engineers can trigger builds and check status through a chat interface.
This spreadsheet has 200 columns of sensor data. Give it a conversational interface so operators can ask 'what's the pressure in tank 3?' instead of hunting through cells.
Our HVAC system has a REST API but no UI. Design an agent wrapper that lets facilities staff control it with plain English requests.
I need to give voice to this dumb printer — let users ask it about toner levels and queue status instead of navigating the horrible web panel.
Take our cron job collection and build an intent-to-action map so people can say 'run the nightly backup' instead of remembering crontab entries.
Set up a conversational wrapper around our Raspberry Pi garage door controller so I can open and close it through the Home Assistant voice assistant.
